texto: Cheap things are real. Cheap things are accessible. Cheap things are made for us because we don’t have any money, and even though we’d like to think we’ll have it someday, we know we don’t now.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nate Ragolia: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Cheap things are real. Cheap things are accessible. Cheap things are made for us because we don’t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, cheap-beer, hipsters pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de hipsters e philosophical.

frase_original: Cheap things are real. Cheap things are accessible. Cheap things are made for us because we don’t have any money, and even though we’d like to think we’ll have it someday, we know we don’t now.

texto: We are, now, just as valuable, intelligent, and real as Eliot or any of his contemporaries. Our ideas and experiences are just as sound and true. Our passion to save our world from what we see it becoming is just as authentic. We are always the Lost Generation, but with different accoutrements.
explicacao: Citação de Nate Ragolia sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “We are, now, just as valuable, intelligent, and real as Eliot or any of his contemporaries.
